title: Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Mandate statistics: data tables
body: " The SAF Mandate statistics are a new set of statistics with the first publication coming out in August 2025. This aligns with the publication cycle of the Renewable Transport Fuel Obligation (RTFO) statistics.  The data tables report on aviation fuel supplied to the UK under the SAF Mandate, which came into effect as of 1 January 2025.  If you have any questions, please contact: environment.stats@dft.gov.uk.   The tables include information on the:    amount of UK aviation fuel from low carbon and fossil fuel   number of SAF Certificates (SAFCs) which have been issued to fuel meeting the sustainability requirements   balance of SAFCs by obligation period   trades of SAFCs between suppliers and traders   carbon and sustainability characteristics of the SAF to which SAFCs have been issued   voluntary scheme data of SAF    2025     Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Mandate statistics 2025: Second provisional report data tables SAF_01 (SAF Mandate tables)  (ODS, 19.7 KB)  This is the second provisional report for 2025 and contains an incomplete dataset for the year based on data available as of 27 October 2025. The complete and finalised dataset will be published in November 2026 alongside the final report for 2025.   Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Mandate statistics 2025: First provisional report data tables SAF_01 (SAF Mandate tables)   (ODS, 10.3 KB)  This is the first provisional report for 2025 and contains an incomplete dataset for the year based on data available as of 31 July 2025. The complete and finalised dataset will be published in November 2026 alongside the final report for 2025.  Accessibility of tables    The department is currently working to make our tables accessible for our users. The data tables for these statistics are now accessible.    We would welcome any feedback on the accessibility of our tables, please email environment.stats@dft.gov.uk.    Contact us     Sustainable Aviation Fuel Mandate statistics     Email environment.stats@dft.gov.uk         "
